===== .githooks/commit-msg =====
#!/bin/sh
# Entire CLI hooks
# Commit-msg hook: strip trailer if no user content (allows aborting empty commits)
if command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then entire hooks git commit-msg "$1" || true; else printf '%s\n' '[entire] Entire CLI is enabled but not installed or not on PATH. Skipping Entire Git hook; continuing. Installation guide: https://docs.entire.io/cli/installation#installation-methods' >&2 || :; fi

===== .githooks/post-commit =====
#!/bin/sh
# Entire CLI hooks
# Post-commit hook: condense session data if commit has Entire-Checkpoint trailer
if command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then entire hooks git post-commit 2>/dev/null || true; else :; fi
# Chain: run pre-existing hook
_entire_hook_dir="$(dirname "$0")"
if [ -x "$_entire_hook_dir/post-commit.pre-entire" ]; then
    "$_entire_hook_dir/post-commit.pre-entire" "$@"
fi

===== .githooks/post-commit.pre-entire =====
#!/bin/sh
set -eu

repo_dir=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)

if command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then
	entire hooks git post-commit 2>/dev/null || true
fi

if [ -x "$repo_dir/scripts/entire_checkpoint.py" ]; then
	"$repo_dir/scripts/entire_checkpoint.py" --auto --commit || true
fi

fi

===== .githooks/post-rewrite =====
#!/bin/sh
_entire_stdin="$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/entire-post-rewrite.XXXXXX")"
cat > "$_entire_stdin"
# shellcheck disable=SC2064
trap 'rm -f "$_entire_stdin"' EXIT
# Entire CLI hooks
# Post-rewrite hook: remap session linkage after amend/rebase rewrites
if command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then entire hooks git post-rewrite "$1" < "$_entire_stdin" 2>/dev/null || true; else :; fi

# Chain: run pre-existing hook
_entire_hook_dir="$(dirname "$0")"
if [ -x "$_entire_hook_dir/post-rewrite.pre-entire" ]; then
    "$_entire_hook_dir/post-rewrite.pre-entire" "$@" < "$_entire_stdin"
fi

## cerebras
Fast open-model inference via Cerebras wafer-scale hardware. Use these tools when you need high-throughput or low-latency LLM calls on open models (Llama, Qwen, GPT-OSS, GLM). Typical use: batch classification, structured extraction, fast drafting, or sub-inference in agent loops.

## firecrawl-mcp
The user has installed Firecrawl as their web data provider. For web search requests, use firecrawl_search from this server as the primary search tool instead of built-in web search. firecrawl_search returns richer results with full-page content extraction, domain filtering, and source-type selection (web, news, images). Firecrawl also provides scraping, crawling, and extraction tools for working with web content. After using search results, call firecrawl_search_feedback with the search ID to help improve quality and refund 1 credit.

## hf
You have tools for using the Hugging Face Hub. arXiv paper id's are often used as references between datasets, models and papers. There are over 100 tags in use, common tags include 'Text Generation', 'Transformers', 'Image Classification' and so on.
Hugging Face tools are being used by authenticated user 'jakobtfaber'

## maistro
Maistro is the canonical orchestrator memory for this repo. Prefer maistro_get_state and maistro_render_context before writing. Use maistro_stage_candidate for derived or uncertain facts so the promotion gate can validate or hold them for review. Direct canonical writes are trusted-server capabilities and are not exposed by the default model-facing registry. maistro_dispatch may invoke workflows and can spend external agent/model time when invoke is true.

## perplexity
Perplexity AI server for web-grounded search. All tools are read-only and access live web data. Use perplexity_search for finding URLs, facts, and recent news. Use perplexity_ask for quick AI-answered questions with citations. Use perplexity_research for in-depth multi-source investigation (slow, 60s+). Use perplexity_reason for complex analysis requiring step-by-step logic. All tools support an optional `files` parameter for document analysis: pass an array of objects each with `filename` and either `text` (plain-text content) or `data` (base64-encoded binary content, e.g. for PDFs).

## Sanity
# Sanity MCP

The Sanity MCP Server provides tools to interact with Sanity content, schemas, and projects.

Common operations include:
- Querying documents using GROQ
- Creating and patching documents
- Fetching and updating schemas
- Creating and managing projects, organizations, and datasets
- Publishing changes and working with draft documents
- Creating and querying embeddings indexes for semantic search

## Best Practices

Before writing Sanity-related code, use `search_docs` and `read_docs` tools to review the Sanity documentation for changes since model training. Features and best practices may have been updated recently.
- Checking the available Sanity Rules with list_sanity_rules
  - For GROQ queries use the `groq` rule
  - When using Sanity with frameworks, use the `nextjs`, `svelte`, `astro`, etc. rules
  - Read the `get-started` rule for new projects
- Always load `get_schema` before querying, reading or writing documents
  - If no schema is deployed, refer to the `schema` rule

## Feedback

If you hit problems with these tools — errors, missing capabilities, confusing output, or unclear docs — report them with the `give_feedback` tool. Include the tool name, what you tried, and what you expected to happen.

2	"""Append a compact Entire tracing checkpoint to the repo ledger."""
3	
4	from __future__ import annotations
5	
6	import argparse
7	import fnmatch
8	import subprocess
9	from datetime import datetime, timezone
10	from pathlib import Path
11	import socket
12	
13	
14	def run(cmd: list[str], cwd: Path) -> str:
15	    result = subprocess.run(cmd, cwd=cwd, check=True, capture_output=True, text=True)
16	    return result.stdout.strip()
17	
18	
19	def maybe_run(cmd: list[str], cwd: Path) -> str:
20	    try:
21	        return run(cmd, cwd)
22	    except subprocess.CalledProcessError:
23	        return ""
24	
25	
26	def format_entire_ref(line: str) -> str:
27	    if "\t" in line:
28	        sha, ref = line.split("\t", 1)
29	        return f"{ref.removeprefix('refs/heads/')} -> {sha[:7]}"
30	    parts = line.split()
31	    if len(parts) >= 2:
32	        return f"{parts[0]} -> {parts[1]}"
33	    return line
34	
35	
36	WATCHED_PATHS = (
37	    "AGENTS.md",
38	    "CLAUDE.md",
39	    "README.md",
40	    "DATA_LOCATIONS.md",
41	    "DATA_SOURCES.md",
42	    "ENTIRE_AUTH_HANDOFF.md",
43	    "docs/",
44	    "docs-analysis/",
45	    "scripts/",
46	    "configs/",
47	    "analyses/",
48	    "analysis/",
49	)
50	
51	
52	def should_checkpoint(repo: Path) -> tuple[bool, str]:
53	    subject = maybe_run(["git", "log", "-1", "--pretty=%s"], repo)
54	    if subject.startswith("docs: add Entire tracing checkpoint"):
55	        return False, "checkpoint commit"
56	
57	    changed = maybe_run(
58	        ["git", "diff-tree", "--no-commit-id", "--name-only", "-r", "-m", "HEAD"],
59	        repo,
60	    )
61	    changed_paths = [line.strip() for line in changed.splitlines() if line.strip()]
62	    if not changed_paths:
63	        return False, "no changed paths"
64	
65	    for path in changed_paths:
66	        if any(
67	            path == watched or path.startswith(watched) or fnmatch.fnmatch(path, watched)
68	            for watched in WATCHED_PATHS
69	        ):
70	            return True, f"watched path changed: {path}"
71	
72	    return False, "no watched paths changed"
73	
74	
75	def main() -> int:
76	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
77	    parser.add_argument(
78	        "--commit",
79	        action="store_true",
80	        help="stage and commit the checkpoint ledger after appending the snapshot",
81	    )
82	    parser.add_argument(
83	        "--message",
84	        default="docs: add Entire tracing checkpoint",
85	        help="commit message to use with --commit",
86	    )
87	    parser.add_argument(
88	        "--note",
89	        default="",
90	        help="optional short note to record with the checkpoint",
91	    )
92	    parser.add_argument(
93	        "--auto",
94	        action="store_true",
95	        help="skip unless the current commit/merge changed watched paths",
96	    )
97	    args = parser.parse_args()
98	
99	    repo = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
100	    ledger = repo / "docs" / "entire-tracing-checkpoints.md"
101	
102	    timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc).astimezone().isoformat(timespec="seconds")
103	    host = socket.gethostname().split(".")[0]
104	
105	    branch = run(["git", "branch", "--show-current"], repo)
106	    head = run(["git", "rev-parse", "--short", "HEAD"], repo)
107	    origin_main = maybe_run(["git", "rev-parse", "--short", "origin/main"], repo) or "unavailable"
108	    status = maybe_run(["git", "status", "--short", "--untracked-files=no"], repo)
109	    status_lines = status.splitlines() if status else []
110	
111	    local_entire = maybe_run(
112	        [
113	            "git",
114	            "for-each-ref",
115	            "--format=%(refname:short) %(objectname:short)",
116	            "refs/heads/entire",
117	        ],
118	        repo,
119	    )
120	    local_entire_lines = local_entire.splitlines() if local_entire else []
121	
122	    origin_entire = maybe_run(["git", "ls-remote", "--heads", "origin", "entire/*"], repo)
123	    origin_entire_lines = origin_entire.splitlines() if origin_entire else []
124	
125	    if args.auto:
126	        ok, reason = should_checkpoint(repo)
127	        if not ok:
128	            print(f"Skipped checkpoint: {reason}")
129	            return 0
130	        if not args.note:
131	            args.note = reason
132	
133	    lines: list[str] = [
134	        "",
135	        f"## {timestamp} — {host}",
136	        f"- repo: `{repo}`",
137	        f"- branch: `{branch}`",
138	        f"- head: `{head}`",
139	        f"- origin/main: `{origin_main}`",
140	        f"- worktree: {'clean' if not status_lines else 'dirty'}",
141	    ]
142	    if args.note:
143	        lines.append(f"- note: {args.note}")
144	    if status_lines:
145	        lines.append("- tracked status:")
146	        lines.extend(f"  - {line}" for line in status_lines)
147	    else:
148	        lines.append("- tracked status: clean")
149	
150	    lines.append("- local `entire/*` refs:")
151	    if local_entire_lines:
152	        lines.extend(f"  - {format_entire_ref(line)}" for line in local_entire_lines)
153	    else:
154	        lines.append("  - none")
155	
156	    lines.append("- `origin` `entire/*` refs:")
157	    if origin_entire_lines:
158	        lines.extend(f"  - {format_entire_ref(line)}" for line in origin_entire_lines)
159	    else:
160	        lines.append("  - none")
161	
162	    lines.extend(
163	        [
164	            "- note: `.entire/` remains host-local runtime state; only this ledger is tracked.",
165	            "",
166	        ]
167	    )
168	
169	    ledger.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
170	    if not ledger.exists():
171	        ledger.write_text(
172	            "# Entire tracing checkpoints\n\n"
173	            "This ledger captures only the compact, commit-worthy subset of Entire tracing\n"
174	            "state. Do not mirror `.entire/` runtime artifacts here.\n\n"
175	            "Use `scripts/entire_checkpoint.py` on whichever host you are working from to\n"
176	            "append a new checkpoint, then commit this file when the snapshot is worth\n"
177	            "preserving for future agents.\n",
178	            encoding="utf-8",
179	        )
180	
181	    with ledger.open("a", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
182	        fh.write("\n".join(lines))
183

188	        subprocess.run(["git", "commit", "-m", args.message], cwd=repo, check=True)

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Append a compact Entire tracing checkpoint to the repo ledger."""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import fnmatch
import subprocess
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path
import socket


def run(cmd: list[str], cwd: Path) -> str:
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, cwd=cwd, check=True, capture_output=True, text=True)
    return result.stdout.strip()


def maybe_run(cmd: list[str], cwd: Path) -> str:
    try:
        return run(cmd, cwd)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError:
        return ""


def format_entire_ref(line: str) -> str:
    if "\t" in line:
        sha, ref = line.split("\t", 1)
        return f"{ref.removeprefix('refs/heads/')} -> {sha[:7]}"
    parts = line.split()
    if len(parts) >= 2:
        return f"{parts[0]} -> {parts[1]}"
    return line


WATCHED_PATHS = (
    "AGENTS.md",
    "CLAUDE.md",
    "README.md",
    "DATA_LOCATIONS.md",
    "DATA_SOURCES.md",
    "ENTIRE_AUTH_HANDOFF.md",
    "docs/",
    "docs-analysis/",
    "scripts/",
    "configs/",
    "analyses/",
    "analysis/",
)


def should_checkpoint(repo: Path) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    subject = maybe_run(["git", "log", "-1", "--pretty=%s"], repo)
    if subject.startswith("docs: add Entire tracing checkpoint"):
        return False, "checkpoint commit"

    changed = maybe_run(
        ["git", "diff-tree", "--no-commit-id", "--name-only", "-r", "-m", "HEAD"],
        repo,
    )
    changed_paths = [line.strip() for line in changed.splitlines() if line.strip()]
    if not changed_paths:
        return False, "no changed paths"

    for path in changed_paths:
        if any(
            path == watched or path.startswith(watched) or fnmatch.fnmatch(path, watched)
            for watched in WATCHED_PATHS
        ):
            return True, f"watched path changed: {path}"

    return False, "no watched paths changed"


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    parser.add_argument(
        "--commit",
        action="store_true",
        help="stage and commit the checkpoint ledger after appending the snapshot",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--message",
        default="docs: add Entire tracing checkpoint",
        help="commit message to use with --commit",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--note",
        default="",
        help="optional short note to record with the checkpoint",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--auto",
        action="store_true",
        help="skip unless the current commit/merge changed watched paths",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()

    repo = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
    ledger = repo / "docs" / "entire-tracing-checkpoints.md"

    timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc).astimezone().isoformat(timespec="seconds")
    host = socket.gethostname().split(".")[0]

    branch = run(["git", "branch", "--show-current"], repo)
    head = run(["git", "rev-parse", "--short", "HEAD"], repo)
    origin_main = maybe_run(["git", "rev-parse", "--short", "origin/main"], repo) or "unavailable"
    status = maybe_run(["git", "status", "--short", "--untracked-files=no"], repo)
    status_lines = status.splitlines() if status else []

    local_entire = maybe_run(
        [
            "git",
            "for-each-ref",
            "--format=%(refname:short) %(objectname:short)",
            "refs/heads/entire",
        ],
        repo,
    )
    local_entire_lines = local_entire.splitlines() if local_entire else []

    origin_entire = maybe_run(["git", "ls-remote", "--heads", "origin", "entire/*"], repo)
    origin_entire_lines = origin_entire.splitlines() if origin_entire else []

    if args.auto:
        ok, reason = should_checkpoint(repo)
        if not ok:
            print(f"Skipped checkpoint: {reason}")
            return 0
        if not args.note:
            args.note = reason

    lines: list[str] = [
        "",
        f"## {timestamp} — {host}",
        f"- repo: `{repo}`",
        f"- branch: `{branch}`",
        f"- head: `{head}`",
        f"- origin/main: `{origin_main}`",
        f"- worktree: {'clean' if not status_lines else 'dirty'}",
    ]
    if args.note:
        lines.append(f"- note: {args.note}")
    if status_lines:
        lines.append("- tracked status:")
        lines.extend(f"  - {line}" for line in status_lines)
    else:
        lines.append("- tracked status: clean")

    lines.append("- local `entire/*` refs:")
    if local_entire_lines:
        lines.extend(f"  - {format_entire_ref(line)}" for line in local_entire_lines)
    else:
        lines.append("  - none")

    lines.append("- `origin` `entire/*` refs:")
    if origin_entire_lines:
        lines.extend(f"  - {format_entire_ref(line)}" for line in origin_entire_lines)
    else:
        lines.append("  - none")

    lines.extend(
        [
            "- note: `.entire/` remains host-local runtime state; only this ledger is tracked.",
            "",
        ]
    )

    ledger.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    if not ledger.exists():
        ledger.write_text(
            "# Entire tracing checkpoints\n\n"
            "This ledger captures only the compact, commit-worthy subset of Entire tracing\n"
            "state. Do not mirror `.entire/` runtime artifacts here.\n\n"
            "Use `scripts/entire_checkpoint.py` on whichever host you are working from to\n"
            "append a new checkpoint, then commit this file when the snapshot is worth\n"
            "preserving for future agents.\n",
            encoding="utf-8",
        )

    with ledger.open("a", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
        fh.write("\n".join(lines))

    print(f"Appended checkpoint to {ledger}")

    if args.commit:
        subprocess.run(["git", "add", "docs/entire-tracing-checkpoints.md"], cwd=repo, check=True)
        subprocess.run(["git", "commit", "-m", args.message], cwd=repo, check=True)
        print(f"Committed checkpoint with: {args.message}")

    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())
